message:
feat(core): 2-tier semantic search infrastructure (phases 1-7)

Add complete search module with embedder traits, hash embedder fallback,
FTVI vector index format, RRF hybrid fusion, reranker trait, and stubs
for Model2Vec/FastEmbed/daemon. Includes SearchConfig, storage migration
v22 (segment_embeddings table), and robot_types semantic fields.

56 tests passing across all search submodules.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"wa-0je","title":"[EPIC] Phase 1 Testing: Unit tests, integration tests, E2E with detailed logging","description":"# Phase 1 Testing Epic

## Purpose
Comprehensive testing infrastructure for Phase 1 components. Every component MUST have thorough tests before Phase 1 can be considered complete.

## Testing Philosophy
- **Unit tests**: Test each module in isolation with mocks
- **Integration tests**: Test component interactions
- **E2E test scripts**: Shell scripts that test real behavior with detailed logging
- **Golden corpus**: Regression fixtures for pattern matching
- **Property-based tests**: Use proptest for invariant verification

## Components to Test

### 1. Workspace & Crate Scaffolding (wa-4vx.1)
- [ ] Cargo workspace compiles successfully
- [ ] All lints pass (clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings)
- [ ] Formatting verified (cargo fmt --check)
- [ ] No unsafe code (compile-time forbid)

### 2. WezTerm Interface Layer (wa-4vx.2)
- [ ] Unit tests for CLI command builders
- [ ] Mock tests for response parsing (list_panes, get_text)
- [ ] Error handling tests (pane not found, socket errors)
- [ ] Integration tests with fixture JSON responses
- [ ] E2E script: `scripts/test_wezterm_interface.sh`

### 3. Storage & Indexing (wa-4vx.3)
- [ ] Schema migration tests
- [ ] Unit tests for StorageHandle operations
- [ ] FTS5 search correctness tests
- [ ] Segment sequence monotonicity tests
- [ ] Gap detection and recording tests
- [ ] Concurrent read/write tests (WAL mode verification)
- [ ] Property-based tests: inserted text is searchable

### 4. Ingest Pipeline (wa-4vx.4)
- [ ] Delta extraction correctness tests
- [ ] Overlap matching algorithm tests
- [ ] Adaptive polling behavior tests
- [ ] Backpressure and bounded channel tests
- [ ] Gap event emission tests
- [ ] Property-based: segments in sequence

### 5. Pattern Detection Engine (wa-4vx.5)
- [ ] Quick reject performance tests
- [ ] Individual pattern rule tests (positive + negative)
- [ ] Golden corpus regression fixtures:
  - tests/corpus/codex/*.txt + *.expect.json
  - tests/corpus/claude_code/*.txt + *.expect.json
  - tests/corpus/gemini/*.txt + *.expect.json
- [ ] Extraction correctness tests (named captures)
- [ ] Performance budgets: p50 < 1ms, p99 < 5ms

## E2E Test Script Requirements
All E2E scripts must:
1. Print timestamped log messages
2. Report PASS/FAIL for each test case
3. Save detailed logs to /tmp/wa_e2e_*.log
4. Exit 0 on all pass, non-zero on any failure
5. Support --verbose flag for extra detail

## Directory Structure
```
tests/
├── unit/
│   ├── wezterm_client_tests.rs
│   ├── storage_tests.rs
│   ├── ingest_tests.rs
│   └── pattern_tests.rs
├── integration/
│   ├── storage_integration.rs
│   └── pipeline_integration.rs
├── corpus/
│   ├── codex/
│   ├── claude_code/
│   └── gemini/
scripts/
├── e2e_test.sh (master script)
├── test_wezterm_interface.sh
├── test_storage.sh
├── test_ingest.sh
└── test_patterns.sh
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- All unit tests pass: `cargo test --all-targets`
- All E2E scripts pass: `./scripts/e2e_test.sh`
- Test coverage > 70% for core modules
- No regressions in golden corpus
- Performance budgets met

 {"id":"wa-0mx7","title":"WeztermInterface trait: abstraction layer for real and mock implementations","description":"# WeztermInterface trait

## Purpose
Create an abstraction layer over WezTerm interactions that allows swapping between real CLI and mock implementations.

## Current State
wa-core directly calls WezTerm CLI commands. This needs to be abstracted.

## Interface Design
```rust
#[async_trait]
pub trait WeztermInterface: Send + Sync {
    /// List all panes across all windows
    async fn list_panes(&self) -> Result<Vec<PaneInfo>>;
    
    /// Get text from a pane's scrollback
    async fn get_text(&self, pane_id: u64, options: &GetTextOptions) -> Result<String>;
    
    /// Send text to a pane (keystrokes)
    async fn send_text(&self, pane_id: u64, text: &str) -> Result<()>;
    
    /// Spawn a new pane
    async fn spawn(&self, options: &SpawnOptions) -> Result<PaneInfo>;
    
    /// Split a pane
    async fn split(&self, pane_id: u64, direction: SplitDirection) -> Result<PaneInfo>;
    
    /// Activate (focus) a pane
    async fn activate(&self, pane_id: u64) -> Result<()>;
    
    /// Get pane info by ID
    async fn get_pane(&self, pane_id: u64) -> Result<Option<PaneInfo>>;
}

pub struct GetTextOptions {
    pub start_line: Option<i64>,
    pub end_line: Option<i64>,
    pub escapes: bool,
}

pub struct SpawnOptions {
    pub domain: Option<String>,
    pub cwd: Option<PathBuf>,
    pub command: Option<Vec<String>>,
}

pub enum SplitDirection {
    Horizontal,
    Vertical,
}
```

## Real Implementation
```rust
pub struct WeztermCli {
    socket_path: Option<PathBuf>,
    timeout: Duration,
}

#[async_trait]
impl WeztermInterface for WeztermCli {
    async fn list_panes(&self) -> Result<Vec<PaneInfo>> {
        let output = Command::new("wezterm")
            .args(["cli", "list", "--format", "json"])
            .output()
            .await?;
        // Parse JSON...
    }
    // ... other methods
}
```

## Factory Pattern
```rust
pub fn create_wezterm(config: &Config) -> Box<dyn WeztermInterface> {
    if config.simulate {
        Box::new(MockWezterm::new())
    } else {
        Box::new(WeztermCli::new(&config.wezterm))
    }
}
```

## Migration Path
1. Define trait
2. Implement for existing WeztermCli
3. Update callers to use trait
4. Implement MockWezterm (separate task)

## Testing
- Trait method contract tests
- Real implementation tests (with WezTerm)
- Factory tests

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] WeztermInterface trait covers all operations
- [ ] WeztermCli implements trait
- [ ] All existing code migrated to use trait
- [ ] Factory creates appropriate implementation
- [ ] Tests verify trait contract","notes":"Migrated remaining WeztermClient call sites in wa-core/wa to WeztermHandle (default_wezterm_handle) + WeztermHandleSource for waits. WorkflowRunner/WorkflowContext now use PolicyGatedInjector<WeztermHandle> alias; tests updated. WeztermInterface gains circuit_status(). cargo fmt/check/clippy/test all pass.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-18T17:52:49.54500413Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-11T00:47:48.217143-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-07T02:22:29.525376256Z"}

 {"id":"wa-0z88","title":"Docs: incident bundles + wa reproduce (shareable support workflow)","description":"# Task: Document incident bundles and `wa reproduce`

## Goal
Make incident bundles discoverable and usable by humans and by support processes.

## Requirements
- Document:
  - when to run `wa reproduce`
  - what data is included by default (and what is not)
  - privacy implications + opt-in flags
  - how to replay a bundle
  - how to attach to a GitHub issue (or share internally)
- Include examples of:
  - a policy denial bundle
  - a rule replay bundle
  - a watcher crash bundle

## Acceptance Criteria
- A new contributor can follow docs to generate and replay a bundle without guesswork.

- Apply capped backoff before restart
- Emit structured crash-loop events

## Success Criteria
- Backoff increases on repeated failures
- Crash loop events are observable","notes":"Audited crates/wa/src/main.rs: crash-loop backoff + structured logging already present in HEAD (run_watcher_with_backoff, crash window/threshold, non-retryable lock errors). No code diff needed; baseline build errors remain (NotificationConfig missing email + rsplit_whitespace typo).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"HazyIsland","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:12:43.174712436Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T00:47:48.232315-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-09T10:18:51.87540251Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-jkq8","depends_on_id":"wa-tm40","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:23Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"wa-jl5","title":"E2E script: notification webhook delivery (mock server + retry + throttle)","description":"# E2E script: notification webhook delivery (mock server + retry + throttle)

## Goal
Prove end-to-end that webhook notifications:
- are emitted for matching events
- are delivered to configured endpoints
- retry with backoff on failure
- respect throttling limits
- recover cleanly from temporary network/server failure

This validates the notification system (`wa-psm`) in real wiring, under the shared E2E contract.

## Key constraints
- Deterministic synchronization: **no fixed `sleep N`** waits.
- Safe-by-default artifacts: payloads must be redacted and must not include transcript contents.
- Uses the standard harness contract (`wa-4vx.10.6`) and runner (`wa-4vx.10.11`).

## Test setup
- Start a local mock webhook server (owned by the E2E harness) with:
  - configurable response sequence (e.g., 500, 500, 200)
  - a `/received` endpoint that returns received payloads as JSON
  - a `/attempt_count` endpoint
  - request logging to an artifacts file

- Start `wa watch` in a dedicated E2E workspace with:
  - notifications configured to point at the mock server
  - a deterministic event source (dummy panes) that can emit known patterns

## Scenarios

### 1) Successful delivery
```bash
# Configure mock to return 200 OK
mock_server --responses 200 &

# Trigger an event that should send notification (via dummy pane output)
wa e2e emit --pane 3 --event codex.usage_limit_reached

# Wait until mock server has >=1 received payload (poll with timeout)
mock_server wait --received-min 1 --timeout 10s

# Assert payload shape + key fields
mock_server dump --received > artifacts/notifications_received.json
jq -e '.[-1].event_type == "codex.usage_limit_reached"' artifacts/notifications_received.json
```

### 2) Retry on failure (backoff)
```bash
# Fail first 2, then succeed
mock_server --responses 500,500,200 &

wa e2e emit --pane 3 --event session.compaction

# Wait until attempt_count >= 3 (poll with timeout)
mock_server wait --attempts-min 3 --timeout 30s

# Assert delivered exactly once (or at least once, depending on idempotency rules)
mock_server dump --received > artifacts/notifications_received.json
jq -e 'map(select(.event_type=="session.compaction")) | length >= 1' artifacts/notifications_received.json
```

### 3) Throttling
```bash
# Configure low throttle limit via an E2E config file for the workspace
# (avoid mutating global user config)

wa e2e emit --pane 3 --event session.compaction --repeat 5 --burst

# Wait for ingestion + notification processing to quiesce (bounded)
wa e2e wait-quiescent --timeout 20s

# Assert at most N delivered in the throttle window
mock_server dump --received > artifacts/notifications_received.json
jq -e 'length <= 2' artifacts/notifications_received.json
```

### 4) Network failure recovery
```bash
# Start with server down
wa e2e emit --pane 3 --event session.compaction

# Bring server up
mock_server --responses 200 &

# Wait for delivery
mock_server wait --received-min 1 --timeout 30s
```

## Artifacts
- `mock_server.log` (request log + timestamps)
- `notifications_received.json` (redacted payloads)
- `wa_watch.log` (watcher logs)
- `events.jsonl` (event evidence)
- `policy_audit_slice.jsonl` (proof we did not leak secrets)

## Registry
- Register this case in the E2E case registry (`wa-4vx.10.20`), including prerequisites and default inclusion status.
- Ensure `./scripts/e2e_test.sh --all` includes this case (or mark non-default with justification).

## Logging contract
- All steps log:
  - test case name
  - watcher/workspace identifiers
  - mock server bind addr
  - timeouts + outcomes

Example:
```
[NOTIFY_E2E] mock_server started addr=127.0.0.1:8080 responses=500,500,200
[NOTIFY_E2E] emit event=session.compaction pane=3
[NOTIFY_E2E] wait attempts>=3 timeout=30s
[NOTIFY_E2E] delivered event=session.compaction attempts=3
```

## Testing
- E2E determinism requirements:
  - no fixed sleeps
  - explicit timeouts on every wait
  - fail with an actionable summary and artifact pointers

- Payload safety requirements:
  - assert payloads contain no raw pane transcripts
  - assert payloads contain no secret-like strings (run the standard redaction scan used by `wa-4vx.10.18`)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Notifications are delivered to the mock webhook for a known event.
- [ ] Retry/backoff behavior is observable (attempt_count increases, eventual delivery).
- [ ] Throttling limits deliveries deterministically.
- [ ] Temporary server unavailability recovers and delivers queued notifications.
- [ ] Artifacts are produced and contain enough context to debug failures.

 {"id":"wa-jquz","title":"Implement step: select next OpenAI account via caut (percent remaining + LRU tie-break)","description":"# Task: Select next OpenAI account via caut

## Goal
Choose the best account to fail over to when usage is exhausted, using `caut` as the source of truth.

## Mechanism
- Refresh usage:
  - call `caut refresh --service openai --format json` (or wrapper equivalent)
- Apply selection policy from `wa-nu4.1.5.2`:
  - filter by configured minimum percent remaining
  - pick highest remaining
  - tie-break by least recently used

## Persistence
- Store chosen account in workflow context.
- Update `accounts.last_used_at` only after successful completion of the failover.

## Testing
- Unit tests:
  - selection is deterministic for a fixed fixture
  - selection explanation is stable (which accounts were filtered and why)
- Integration tests:
  - DB mirror updates are correct

## Acceptance Criteria
- Selection is deterministic given the same caut output.
- Accounts below threshold are never selected.

 {"id":"wa-jrcy","title":"Integrate noise control into status/triage outputs and TUI event feed","description":"# Task: Integrate noise control into UX surfaces

## Goal
Ensure noise control is visible and intuitive in user-facing surfaces.

## Requirements
- CLI:
  - status/triage shows deduped events with counts and last_seen
  - muted events are visible in a separate view
- TUI:
  - event feed shows suppression counts
  - quick actions: mute/unmute
- Notifications:
  - include suppression counts and escalation signals

## Testing
- Output tests for stable formatting.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Users can understand what's happening without being spammed.

@@ -88,6 +88,81 @@ pub struct Config {
 
     /// Session snapshot settings (periodic capture, retention)
     pub snapshots: SnapshotConfig,
+
+    /// Semantic search settings (embedding models, fusion, daemon)
+    pub search: SearchConfig,
+}
+
+// =============================================================================
+// Search Config
+// =============================================================================
+
+/// Configuration for 2-tier semantic search.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+#[serde(default)]
+pub struct SearchConfig {
+    /// Enable semantic search features.
+    pub enabled: bool,
+    /// Search mode: "fts5", "lexical", "semantic", "hybrid", "two-tier".
+    pub mode: String,
+    /// Directory for downloaded model files.
+    pub models_dir: String,
+    /// Model name for the fast tier (Model2Vec).
+    pub fast_model: String,
+    /// Model name for the quality tier (MiniLM).
+    pub quality_model: String,

+impl Default for SearchConfig {

+
+/// Configuration for the search daemon background service.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]

+pub struct SearchDaemonConfig {

+    /// Worker scan interval in seconds.

+    /// Batch size for worker embedding jobs.
+    pub worker_batch_size: usize,
+}
+
+impl Default for SearchDaemonConfig {
+    fn default() -> Self {
+        Self {
+            enabled: false,
+            socket_path: ".ft/search-daemon.sock".into(),
+            auto_spawn: true,
+            worker_scan_interval_secs: 30,
+            worker_batch_size: 64,
+        }
+    }
 }
